Han Unpyeong

1 work in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Nine-Dragon Falls
by Han Unpyeong
Joseon · Han Unpyeong · late 19th c. · Korea
A waterfall cascading in nine tiers down a mountain — Korean late-Joseon ink-and-color painting, the brush loose enough to be mistaken for the water it describes.
←Metalworker unknown Hiroshige, Utagawa→
